title: Ranunculus strongylus
intro: The Ranunculus strongylus, also known as the Buttercup, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and skin conditions due to its high content of bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus strongylus, particularly its flavonoids and alkaloids, are responsible for its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal medicine, Ranunculus strongylus is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Ranunculus strongylus is classified within the family Ranunculaceae. This family is commonly known as the buttercup family and consists of approximately 1,900 species. Ranunculus strongylus is further classified within the genus Ranunculus, which is a group of plants that are mostly perennials and have showy flowers. The genus Ranunculus is divided into several sections, one of which is where Ranunculus strongylus is placed. Within the Ranunculaceae family, Ranunculus strongylus can be identified by its unique characteristics and growth habits.

side_effects: Ranunculus strongylus contains toxic compounds that can cause skin irritation, nausea, and vomiting if ingested.
